description: Structured weekly digest command. Aggregates a full week of git, PR, review, and AI session data into a formatted summary with week-over-week comparison. Optionally writes to file or sends OS notification.

userStories.title: Weekly data aggregation
userStories.description: As a dev, I want `blackbox digest` to gather all activity for the configured week period (Mon-Sun or Sun-Sat based on config), so I can review a complete week at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Calls query_activity with a full-week range (start-of-week midnight local to end-of-week 23:59:59 local)
Week start day is configurable (Monday default, Sunday optional) via Config field `week_start_day`
Defaults to current week; accepts optional `--week <offset>` flag (0=current, -1=last week, -2=two weeks ago)
Returns ActivitySummary with correct period_label e.g. 'Week of Mar 24, 2025'
Handles weeks with zero activity gracefully (empty repos vec, prints 'No activity' message)
Handles partial weeks (e.g. current week only Mon-Wed if today is Wed)

userStories.title: Config field for week start day
userStories.description: As a dev, I want to configure whether my week starts Monday or Sunday, matching my team calendar.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `week_start_day: Option<String>` to Config struct with `#[serde(default)]`
Valid values: 'monday' (default), 'sunday'
Invalid value logs warning and falls back to 'monday'
week_range() updated or new digest_week_range(offset, start_day) fn added in query.rs
Existing `week` command unaffected (still uses hardcoded Monday start)

userStories.title: Week-over-week comparison
userStories.description: As a dev, I want to see how this week compares to last week so I can gauge productivity trends.
acceptanceCriteria: Digest pretty output includes a comparison section showing delta vs previous week
Metrics compared: total_commits, total_estimated_time (minutes), total_reviews, total_ai_session_time
Delta displayed as +/- with color (green=up, red=down, yellow=same) in pretty format
Previous week queried lazily — only when format=pretty or when --compare flag passed for json/csv
If no prior week data exists, comparison section is omitted without error
New struct WeeklyDigest { current: ActivitySummary, previous: Option<ActivitySummary> } in query.rs or digest.rs

userStories.title: Digest pretty output formatter
userStories.description: As a dev, I want a digest-specific pretty format that is more structured than `blackbox week`, showing daily breakdown and highlights.
acceptanceCriteria: Header: 'Weekly Digest — Week of <Mon date> to <Sun date>'
Top-level stats: total time, commits, repos, reviews, AI session time
Per-day breakdown: each day of week listed, with commit count and time estimate for that day (days with no activity shown as '—')
Per-repo section same as existing render_summary_to_string but grouped under repos
Week-over-week comparison block at bottom (if previous week data available)
New function render_digest(digest: &WeeklyDigest) in output.rs
Uses existing format_duration() and colored crate for styling

userStories.title: Digest json/csv output
userStories.description: As a dev, I want json and csv output for digest so I can pipe it to other tools.
acceptanceCriteria: json output: wraps existing JsonSummary for current week, adds optional previous_week field and week_start/week_end ISO timestamps
csv output: same schema as existing render_csv but with period set to the week date range string
Both formats respect --compare flag to include/exclude previous week data
render_digest_json(digest) and render_digest_csv(digest) added to output.rs
JSON includes week_start, week_end as top-level ISO-8601 strings

userStories.title: CLI command: blackbox digest
userStories.description: As a dev, I want `blackbox digest` as a first-class subcommand.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `Digest` variant to Commands enum in cli.rs
Flags: --format (pretty/json/csv, default pretty), --week <i32> (offset, default 0), --output-file <path>, --summarize, --compare (include WoW comparison, default true for pretty)
Add match arm in main.rs
Not exempt from config check (requires DB)
Doc string: 'Show structured weekly digest'

userStories.title: --output-file flag
userStories.description: As a dev, I want to write my digest to a file (e.g. markdown or json) for archiving or sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: --output-file <path> flag on Digest command
If provided, output written to file instead of stdout
Parent directories created with std::fs::create_dir_all if missing
Success message printed to stdout: 'Digest written to <path>'
Error if file write fails (propagate with anyhow context)
Works with all --format values

userStories.title: OS notification delivery
userStories.description: As a dev, I want an optional OS notification with digest summary so I can get weekly recap without running a command.
acceptanceCriteria: --notify flag on Digest command
macOS: uses osascript display notification (no new dep required)
Linux: uses notify-send if available, silently skips if not
Notification body: top-level stats only (commits, time, repos) — max ~100 chars
Notification title: 'Blackbox Weekly Digest'
Failure to send notification is non-fatal (warn to stderr, continue)

userStories.title: Integration tests for digest
userStories.description: As a dev, I want tests covering the digest command so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/digest.rs (or tests/query.rs extended) with tempfile DB
Test: empty week returns 'No activity' message
Test: week range fn respects Monday vs Sunday start
Test: week offset -1 returns prior week range
Test: render_digest pretty output contains expected header and stats
Test: render_digest_json parses cleanly with serde_json
Test: --output-file writes to temp path
CLI integration test via assert_cmd for `blackbox digest --format json`

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
